title: Decision Card v1
description: The durable record of a decision once made — an Architecture Decision Record (ADR) generalized to project decisions. Follows Michael Nygard's core (title, status, context, decision, consequences) with MADR's supplemental fields (decision drivers, options considered). This is distinct from an action-card of type 'decision', which represents a decision that still needs to be DRIVEN; a decision-card is the recorded OUTCOME, and can close such an action-card via resolves_action_card. Lives in <project>/decisions/.

id.description: Format: DEC-{customer_code}-{project_code}-{NNN}. Numbered sequentially per project. Example: DEC-ACME-CLOUD-001.

title.description: Short noun phrase naming the decision, e.g. 'Phased cutover over single-weekend big-bang'.

status.description: ADR lifecycle. proposed=stakeholders have not agreed yet; accepted=agreed and in force; rejected=considered and declined; superseded=replaced by a later decision (set superseded_by); deprecated=no longer applies but not directly replaced.

date.description: Date the decision reached its current status.

cynefin_domain.description: Optional: the Cynefin domain this decision was made in (see reference/cynefin.md). Records the KIND of decision — clear=apply known practice; complicated=analyzable trade-off; complex=resolved via safe-to-fail probes; chaotic=made under crisis to stabilize. Useful when reviewing later why the decision was approached the way it was.

context.description: The forces at play — technical, political, commercial, schedule — stated value-neutrally as facts. Why is a decision needed now? (Nygard 'Context'.)

decision.description: The decision itself, in active voice: 'We will ...'. (Nygard 'Decision'.)

consequences.description: The resulting context after applying the decision — both the benefits and the new constraints/costs it creates. (Nygard 'Consequences'.)

decision_drivers.description: MADR: the factors that most influenced the choice (e.g. 'must clear the data-center backstop', 'minimize business disruption').

options_considered.description: MADR: the alternatives weighed. Recording rejected options is what makes a decision auditable later. Set chosen=true on the one option that matches the `decision` prose (exactly one on an accepted/proposed card; leave all false on a rejected card).

decision_makers.description: Who actually made (or owns) the call. A decision with no named decision-maker is a decision nobody is accountable for.

resolves_action_card.description: The action-card of type 'decision' that this record closes out (the 'decision to be made' that has now been made).

decided_at_meeting.description: The meeting-card where the decision was taken, if applicable.

action_cards.description: Action-cards that follow FROM the decision (the work it sets in motion).

updated.description: Date this card was last touched (distinct from `date`, the decision date).
